reasoning: Both models skipped the PKGBUILD entirely, classifying it as a 'binary file'. No files were actually reviewed by either model, so there is zero evidence on which to base a safety verdict. The PKGBUILD is a plain text shell script and should never be treated as binary — this is a tooling-level file-type detection bug, not a property of the package. With no content analysis performed, the only honest verdict is inconclusive.
disagreement_analysis: There is no substantive disagreement: both models produced identical 'skipped' results due to the same misclassification of PKGBUILD as a binary file. The trigger note ('shallow, reviewed 0 files') confirms the audit never actually examined any content.
coverage_issues: PKGBUILD was not reviewed by any model — the single most important file for an AUR audit was skipped
No check for accompanying .install hooks or source files (none were enumerated because the audit aborted at the PKGBUILD stage)
re_audit_focus: PKGBUILD (force text-mode reading; inspect source array, build/package/prepare functions and any sha/url sources)
Any .install scripts (post_install/post_upgrade hooks that run fc-cache or similar)
Verify source download URLs and checksums
learnings: Fix the binary-file heuristic: PKGBUILD (and .install, .sh) must always be force-read as text. Misclassifying PKGBUILD as binary causes a total audit failure on the most critical file.
An audit that reviews 0 files should never be allowed to return anything other than 'inconclusive/error' — guard against zero-coverage outcomes producing a misleading clean status.
Add a hardcoded allowlist of known-text filenames/extensions (PKGBUILD, *.install, *.sh, *.patch, *.desktop) that bypass binary detection.
When the initial PKGBUILD safety gate fails due to a tooling issue (not a genuine danger), the message 'doesn't look good' is misleading — distinguish 'could not read' from 'looks dangerous'.
Both models gave identical zero-output results, indicating the failure occurred in the harness before model inference; model selection is irrelevant until the file-reading bug is fixed.
